description: §61.7 — an `<endpoint>` naming a `method=` that is not a recognized HTTP method (here `HEAD`) fires E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID. The recognized set is GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (the §60.2 http-method set, reused).
rationale: §61.7: `method=` SHALL be one of GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE. A value outside that set (HEAD) is E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID (parse-time, W2).
